bibliographic_data.title: Math Search for the Masses: Multimodal Search Interfaces and Appearance-Based Retrieval
bibliographic_data.abstract: We summarize math search engines and search interfaces produced by the Document and Pattern Recognition Lab in recent years, and in particular the min math search interface and the Tangent search engine. Source code for both systems are publicly available. “The Masses” refers to our emphasis on creating systems for mathematical non-experts, who may be looking to define unfamiliar notation, or browse documents based on the visual appearance of formulae rather than their mathematical semantics.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a multimodal math search interface called min that allows users to enter queries using both text and mathematical notation, emphasizing usability for non-experts.
core_content.key_methodology: The min interface uses external web services for symbol recognition and parsing. It supports entering formulae visually through a canvas and keywords in a text box.
outcomes: Created the min math search interface for non-expert users
Emphasized appearance-based retrieval of mathematical notation
core_content.full_text_markdown: Mathematical notation is crucial in modern societies, but can be frustrating or anxiety-inducing for many due to unfamiliarity. The paper proposes a multimodal math search interface called min that allows users to enter queries using both text and mathematical notation, emphasizing usability for non-experts. The min interface uses external web services for symbol recognition and parsing. It supports entering formulae visually through a canvas and keywords in a text box. This approach aims to make it easier for non-experts to search for unfamiliar notation or browse documents based on the visual appearance of formulae rather than their mathematical semantics.
